description_long: This guard rail is a long, slender, and linear barrier typically used along roadsides or medians for safety. The main rail appears to be a continuous, slightly contoured metal beam, likely galvanized steel, with a muted olive-green finish. The profile is relatively thin and flat, with a subtle taper at one end, suggesting a streamlined or tapered end treatment. There are no visible vertical posts in the provided views, but the rail's length and uniformity imply standard post spacing. The surface is hard and likely smooth, with minimal reflectivity and no visible reflective markings or panels. This object is designed to act as a physical obstacle, preventing vehicles or pedestrians from crossing into restricted or hazardous areas.
description: A long, slender metal guard rail with a muted olive-green finish and a tapered end, designed as a roadside safety barrier.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length and slight taper of the guard rail, with a flat, uniform profile.
description_view_1: End-on view revealing the thin, linear cross-section and minimal width of the rail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the length and subtle taper at the far end.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, again highlighting the narrow profile and straight alignment.
